title: ESM import-order gating for Node version check before dependency load
context: CLI crashes on Node < 22.13 with cryptic ERR_UNKNOWN_BUILTIN_MODULE when import graph tries to load node:sqlite. The engines field is not enforced by npm/npx, so users with old Node can still run the CLI. Must not block the edit-hook subcommand (con_03a0b94b2e).
decision: Create a zero-dependency preflight module and import it first in the entry point. ESM evaluates it before the rest of the graph, allowing Node version check to run before any modules that depend on node:sqlite load. Hook subcommand exits 0 silently; other commands exit 1 with actionable upgrade message.
consequences: Preflight check runs on every CLI invocation with no option to skip
Preflight module must remain dependency-free to avoid the problem it solves
Import order is now critical — comment in code enforces this
Special-case logic for hook subcommand is baked into the version gate
User sees immediate, helpful error message instead of cryptic module error
alternatives_rejected: Rely on engines field in package.json — not enforced by npm/npx
Check Node version after CLI argument parsing — crashes before arguments are parsed
Wrapper shell script or launcher — adds complexity and failure points
Fail hard on hook subcommand — violates constraint that edits must never be blocked

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Rely on engines field in package.json — not enforced by npm/npx
Check Node version after CLI argument parsing — crashes before arguments are parsed
Wrapper shell script or launcher — adds complexity and failure points
Fail hard on hook subcommand — violates constraint that edits must never be blocked
